channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Alcohol warning labels that link drinking to cancer are effective, study says
item.description: A new study finds updated warning labels pointing out the increased risks of specific diseases, like cancer, would do a better job of encouraging people to drink less alcohol than the current warning.
item.content:encoded: A new study finds updated warning labels pointing out the increased risks of specific diseases, like cancer, would do a better job of encouraging people to drink less alcohol than the current warning.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: With legal briefs in, Supreme Court weighs telehealth access for the abortion pill
item.description: After an appeals court tried to end telemedicine access to mifepristone, one of the two pills used in a medication abortion, the Supreme Court stepped in. Here's what's happened and what's to come.
item.content:encoded:
After an appeals court tried to end telemedicine access to mifepristone, one of the two pills used in a medication abortion, the Supreme Court stepped in. Here's what's happened and what's to come.

(Image credit: Jonathan Bachman)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Studying these young Alzheimer's patients led to breakthroughs. Trump cut the funding
item.description: Families with rare gene mutations that cause Alzheimer's in middle age are giving scientists a unique window on the disease, and a quick way to test potential treatments.
item.content:encoded:
Families with rare gene mutations that cause Alzheimer's in middle age are giving scientists a unique window on the disease, and a quick way to test potential treatments.

(Image credit: Juan Diego Reyes for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Psychiatrists say RFK Jr.'s take on SSRIs is an 'oversimplification' of the problem
item.description: The American Psychiatric Association says too few patients can access comprehensive mental health care in the United States. It welcomes new investments in improving access to evidence-based care.
item.content:encoded:
The American Psychiatric Association says too few patients can access comprehensive mental health care in the United States. It welcomes new investments in improving access to evidence-based care.

(Image credit: Rod Lamkey)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: Health officials track dozens who left hantavirus-stricken ship after 1st fatality
item.description: Oceanwide Expeditions has revealed that 30 passengers disembarked from its cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak on April 24, on the remote island of St. Helena, nearly two weeks after the first passenger died.
item.content:encoded:
Oceanwide Expeditions has revealed that 30 passengers disembarked from its cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak on April 24, on the remote island of St. Helena, nearly two weeks after the first passenger died.

(Image credit: Misper Apawu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The FDA has approved the sale of fruit-flavored vapes. What's behind the shift?
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Mitch Zeller, former director of the FDA's Center for Tobacco Products, about the agency's approval of fruit-flavored electronic cigarettes.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Mitch Zeller, former director of the FDA's Center for Tobacco Products, about the agency's approval of fruit-flavored electronic cigarettes.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: These companies help parents try to pick their babies' traits. Experts are wary
item.description: Prospective parents can now pick embryos based on risk predictions for thousands of diseases and odds for specific traits. But should they?
item.content:encoded:
Prospective parents can now pick embryos based on risk predictions for thousands of diseases and odds for specific traits. But should they?

(Image credit: Kate Medley for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Preserving pollinators is good for health -- and income
item.description: Pollinators have economic and health benefits, but those benefits have been difficult to quantify. A new study puts some numbers to how important pollinators are for both nutrition and income.
item.content:encoded: Pollinators have economic and health benefits, but those benefits have been difficult to quantify. A new study puts some numbers to how important pollinators are for both nutrition and income.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Have you lost or are worried about losing SNAP benefits? NPR wants to hear your story
item.description: NPR wants to hear from people impacted by changes to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.
item.content:encoded:
NPR wants to hear from people impacted by changes to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: A new Medicare option for weight loss drugs is coming: Here's what to know
item.description: Millions of people with Medicare will soon be eligible to get discounted GLP-1 drugs for weight loss. Here's how it will work.
item.content:encoded:
Millions of people with Medicare will soon be eligible to get discounted GLP-1 drugs for weight loss. Here's how it will work.

(Image credit: Universal Images Group)
item.dc:creator: Jackie Fortiér
